methodology_sections: 1. Conservation Laws - Solvency, inflation checks, supply integrity
2. Relationship & Ratio Invariants - Collateralization, rate consistency
3. Economic Monotonicity - Yield accumulation, exchange rate direction
4. Incentive & Game Theoretic Integrity - Attack cost vs profit, slashing
5. Fee & Yield Consistency - Fee accounting, yield distribution

invariants.description: A user's share balance must always be greater than or equal to their pending withdrawal requests.
invariants.attack_scenario: If a user can transfer or burn shares that are already locked in the withdrawal queue, they could double-spend their shares, draining the vault's underlying assets and causing insolvency.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_userBalanceCoversWithdrawals(address user) public view { assert(balanceOf(user) >= withdrawRequestsByAddress[user]); }

invariants.description: The vault's base token balance must always be at least the total amount of base tokens waiting for deposit execution.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the contract's base token balance falls below `depositQueue.totalAWFDeposit`, it means base tokens meant for pending deposits have been misappropriated or spent, leading to a failure when executing deposits and a loss of user funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_baseTokenCoversDeposits() public view { assert(baseToken.balanceOf(address(this)) >= depositQueue.totalAWFDeposit); }

invariants.description: The amount of base tokens asked from connectors for withdrawal must not exceed the total amount needed for the current withdraw group.
invariants.attack_scenario: If the manager can ask for more tokens than needed (e.g., due to a missing accumulation check in `retrieveTokensForWithdraw`), they could drain connectors into the accounting manager, breaking the allocation strategy and potentially misappropriating funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_amountAskedForWithdrawBounds() public view { assert(amountAskedForWithdraw <= currentWithdrawGroup.totalCBAmount); }

invariants.description: Performance fee shares waiting for distribution must accurately reflect a sustained profit; if current profit drops below stored profit, pending fees must be zero.
invariants.attack_scenario: If TVL drops but performance fees are still minted based on an old, higher profit (because `checkIfTVLHasDroped` was not called before `collectPerformanceFees`), the strategy manager extracts unearned value, unfairly diluting users.

invariants.description: When a withdraw group is fulfilled, the total available base amount allocated for withdrawals must not exceed the contract's free base token balance.
invariants.attack_scenario: If `totalABAmount` is set higher than available funds (excluding pending deposits), executing withdrawals will either revert or drain funds meant for pending deposits, breaking the queue isolation.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_withdrawGroupAssetConservation() public view { if (currentWithdrawGroup.isFullfilled) { assert(currentWithdrawGroup.totalABAmount <= baseToken.balanceOf(address(this)) - depositQueue.totalAWFDeposit); } }

invariants.description: The minimum health factor for borrowing connectors must never be set below the absolute MINIMUM_HEALTH_FACTOR.
invariants.attack_scenario: A malicious or compromised maintainer could lower the health factor below safe limits, exposing the vault's collateral to liquidation and causing massive losses of user funds.
invariants.solidity_property: function invariant_minimumHealthFactor() public view { assert(minimumHealthFactor >= MINIMUM_HEALTH_FACTOR); }
